title: Funny Bunny Egg Hunt
shortSummary: Spring egg hunt for local children, with pre-registration required and a White Plains High School rain location.
fullDescription:
White Plains Recreation & Parks listed the 2026 Funny Bunny Egg Hunt for Saturday, March 28. The city shared activity hours running from 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. and noted White Plains High School as the rain location.

This is the kind of quick-hit family event residents often look for on mobile, so the rebuilt site treats it as a clean, scan-friendly listing instead of burying it in a long recreation guide.
locationName: White Plains High School North Gym
locationAddress: White Plains High School, White Plains, NY
organizer: City of White Plains Recreation & Parks

title: Common Council Meeting
shortSummary: Open public meeting of the White Plains Common Council at City Hall.
fullDescription:
The White Plains city calendar lists a Common Council meeting for Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. in the Common Council Chamber on the second floor of City Hall.

Including civic meetings alongside festivals and family events helps WPCNA present a fuller picture of neighborhood life, not just entertainment listings.
locationAddress: 255 Main Street, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

title: STEAM Saturday
shortSummary: Hands-on STEAM activities for children in grades 4-8 at the White Plains Public Library.
fullDescription:
White Plains Public Library scheduled STEAM Saturday for Saturday, April 4, 2026 from 12:00 p.m. to 1:00 p.m. in Galaxy Hall.

The library notes that the program is for grades 4-8 and that tickets are distributed starting 30 minutes before the event at the Compass Desk in the Trove.
locationName: White Plains Public Library, Galaxy Hall

title: "Energize U" Energy Efficiency Workshop
shortSummary: Energy-efficiency workshop focused on lowering utility costs, with a free conservation kit and dinner included.
fullDescription:
The White Plains Public Library calendar lists this workshop for Monday, April 6, 2026 from 5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. in the Auditorium.

According to the library, each participant receives a free energy conservation kit. Dinner begins at 5:30 p.m., the presentation starts at 6:00 p.m., and registration is required.

title: English Conversation Group
shortSummary: Free conversation practice session for adults looking to build everyday English skills.
fullDescription:
The White Plains Public Library lists this free conversation group for Thursday, April 9, 2026 from 2:00 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. in the Community Room.

The event description emphasizes a simple goal: helping residents have fun while improving their English conversation skills. No registration is required.
locationName: White Plains Public Library Community Room

title: Catch Me If You Can
shortSummary: Mainstage musical at the White Plains Performing Arts Center, based on the hit film and true story.
fullDescription:
White Plains Performing Arts Center is staging Catch Me If You Can from April 10 through May 3, 2026. The production is described as a high-flying musical comedy built around the Terrence McNally, Marc Shaiman, and Scott Wittman adaptation of the story.

The WPPAC listing shows ticket prices ranging from $53 to $71 and positions the run as one of its mainstage offerings for the spring season.
locationAddress: 11 City Place, 3rd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

title: Downtown White Plains Wing Walk 2026
shortSummary: Ticketed tasting event across downtown restaurants, with wing samples, voting, and a full afternoon of walkable stops.
fullDescription:
The White Plains BID lists Wing Walk 2026 for Saturday, April 25 from 12:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. across downtown White Plains.

The official page frames the event as a day of restaurant-hopping, wing sampling, and friendly competition. Participants taste wings at participating restaurants and vote to help decide the year's champion.
locationAddress: Multiple restaurants in downtown White Plains, NY 10601

title: Downtown White Plains Rock the Block 2026
shortSummary: Free downtown summer street series with live music, outdoor dining, games, and family-friendly activities.
fullDescription:
Rock the Block returns for four Wednesday evenings in 2026: May 20, June 17, July 15, and August 19 from 5:30 p.m. to 8:30 p.m.

The White Plains BID describes it as a car-free transformation of Mamaroneck Avenue into a walkable downtown destination with live music, outdoor dining, mini golf, lawn games, and room for families to linger.
locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

title: WPCNA Neighborhood Workshop
shortSummary: First annual WPCNA workshop for residents working to create or reinvigorate neighborhood associations.
fullDescription:
WPCNA published workshop materials for its first annual neighborhood workshop held on March 11, 2025.

The description on the current WPCNA materials page invites residents to reach out if they need help creating or revitalizing a neighborhood association, making it a useful reference point for future neighborhood leadership programming.
organizer: White Plains Council of Neighborhood Associations

title: Final Vision Zero Public Meeting
shortSummary: Public meeting on the White Plains Vision Zero Action Plan, focused on roadway safety recommendations and next steps.
fullDescription:
WPCNA's archived local-events page listed this Vision Zero public meeting for Thursday, April 24, 2025 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. at the White Plains Public Library.

The meeting agenda centered on infrastructure recommendations, public feedback, and the broader Vision Zero Action Plan designed to reduce severe traffic injuries and fatalities in White Plains.

title: Arbor Day in White Plains
shortSummary: Arbor Day programming shared through WPCNA's event archive and preserved with its flyer for future reference.
fullDescription:
WPCNA archived an Arbor Day flyer for Friday, April 25, 2025 as part of its local events page.

The new site keeps the item in a structured archive so residents can browse the event history without encountering the original page's PDF-heavy clutter.

title: Downtown White Plains Wing Walk 2025
shortSummary: Downtown restaurant tasting event with voting, timed entry windows, and a strong turnout across local wing spots.
fullDescription:
Wing Walk 2025 ran on Saturday, April 26 from 12:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. across downtown White Plains.

WPCNA's listing linked back to the White Plains BID event page, which described a day of tasting and voting across participating restaurants with multiple ticket options and admission windows.
locationAddress: Multiple restaurants in downtown White Plains, NY 10601

title: Rock the Block 2025
shortSummary: Downtown summer series that turned Mamaroneck Avenue into a walkable mix of live music, dining, and family activities.
fullDescription:
The 2025 Rock the Block series ran from May through August with live music, expanded outdoor dining, and family-friendly games on Mamaroneck Avenue.

The archived source material highlights how the series helped define downtown White Plains as a social gathering place, which makes it an especially good example of the kind of recurring event structure this rebuild is designed to support.
locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

title: Block-toberfest
shortSummary: Free fall block party that replaced a weather-canceled Oktoberfest with music, games, vendors, and family activities.
fullDescription:
Block-toberfest took place on Sunday, October 26, 2025 from 1:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. on Mamaroneck Avenue.

The White Plains BID positioned the event as a reworked, weather-resilient version of its usual Oktoberfest programming, mixing live oompah music, outdoor dining, family activities, and seasonal games into one downtown block party.
locationAddress: Mamaroneck Avenue between Maple Avenue and East Post Road, White Plains, NY 10601

title: Downtown White Plains Holiday Market
shortSummary: Indoor holiday market with local vendors in the former Barnes & Noble space at City Center.
fullDescription:
The Downtown White Plains Holiday Market ran from December 5 through December 14, 2025 from 11:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. each day.

WPCNA's source page linked to the White Plains BID listing, which emphasized the move indoors to the former Barnes & Noble space at City Center and featured a broad lineup of vendors and seasonal shopping opportunities.
locationName: White Plains City Center, former Barnes & Noble space
locationAddress: White Plains City Center, White Plains, NY 10601

title: Vision Zero Public Meeting
shortSummary: First public meeting on White Plains roadway safety issues and the Vision Zero Action Plan.
fullDescription:
WPCNA shared this Vision Zero public meeting for Wednesday, October 23, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. at the White Plains Public Library Auditorium.

The archived announcement focused on keeping residents informed, gathering questions and feedback, and encouraging participation in the related safety survey.

title: Vision Zero Action Plan Stakeholder Meeting
shortSummary: Stakeholder session on the White Plains Vision Zero Action Plan, goals, timeline, and preliminary findings.
fullDescription:
The WPCNA local-events archive preserved details for a Vision Zero stakeholder meeting held on Tuesday, September 24, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. in the Common Council Chamber at City Hall.

The meeting agenda included plan goals, process timeline, analysis updates, and a moderated discussion designed to gather stakeholder feedback on roadway safety in White Plains.
locationAddress: 255 Main Street, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601

title: Downtown Revitalization Initiative Public Workshop
shortSummary: Public workshop on downtown improvement priorities, including bike lanes, open space, and public art.
fullDescription:
WPCNA's archived event listing included the second and final Downtown Revitalization Initiative public workshop on Monday, September 9, 2024 from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m.

The workshop invited residents to review potential downtown projects and weigh in on priorities such as open space, public art, and bike-friendly improvements in the heart of White Plains.
locationAddress: 100 Martine Avenue, 2nd Floor, White Plains, NY 10601
